tasks.name: Project Scaffold (Electron + Vite + TS)
tasks.description:
# Task 01 — Project Scaffold (Electron + Vite + TS)

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Stand up the foundational Electron + Vite + TypeScript project skeleton for the War Room v2 HUD, targeting macOS Apple Silicon.

## Scope
- Electron main + preload + renderer split; contextIsolation on, nodeIntegration off.
- Vite + React 18 + TypeScript renderer (plain Vite, not electron-vite).
- ESLint + Prettier + tsconfig strict; folder structure: src/main, src/preload, src/renderer, src/shared.
- npm scripts: dev, build, lint, package. A minimal window that loads the renderer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `npm run build` passes clean.
- `npm run dev` launches an Electron window rendering a placeholder HUD root.
- Repo structure documented in README-DEV.md.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-01`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/*-warroom.json`, never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Design System & TNFG Theme Tokens
tasks.description:
# Task 02 — Design System & TNFG Theme Tokens

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Establish the visual design system and TNFG theme tokens used by every panel and primitive.

## Scope
- CSS variables / theme tokens for the TNFG palette, spacing, typography, z-layers, glow/bloom.
- Reusable primitives: Card/Panel frame, TitleBar, StatPill, DonutBase, TrendLine wrappers.
- Dark near-black base with teal/cyan accents and red alert state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Theme tokens centralized in one module; no hardcoded colors elsewhere.
- A Storybook-style or /dev route renders the primitives for visual QA.
- Crisp at 4K.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-02`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/*-warroom.json`, never hardcoded.

tasks.name: HUD Aesthetic Primitives (orb, reticles, scanlines, bloom)
tasks.description:
# Task 03 — HUD Aesthetic Primitives (orb, reticles, scanlines, bloom)

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Build the signature 'Jarvis-in-TNFG-teal' aesthetic primitives, chiefly the animated Vera Orb.

## Scope
- Animated Orb: particle/energy core + rotating radar rings + tick marks; states idle/listening/thinking/speaking/alert/fullscreen.
- Canvas/WebGL or performant CSS; corner reticles, scanlines, grid backdrop, neon bloom overlay.
- Orb exposes a state API the protocol layer can drive later.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Orb renders smoothly (>=50fps) at 4K; all six states visually distinct.
- Reticle/scanline/grid/bloom overlay composable behind panels.
- Screenshot captured for review.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-03`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Fullscreen Kiosk Window + Crash Auto-Recover
tasks.description:
# Task 04 — Fullscreen Kiosk Window + Crash Auto-Recover

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Configure the Electron window for fullscreen kiosk operation with crash auto-recovery.

## Scope
- Fullscreen, no chrome/menu; hide cursor after idle; prevent accidental quit.
- Crash/renderer-gone handler that reloads; main-process supervisor.
- Discreet admin/exit hotkey (e.g. Cmd+Shift+Esc) — configurable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- App launches fullscreen with no chrome/dock/cursor.
- Killing the renderer auto-recovers within a few seconds.
- Admin hotkey reveals exit; normal keys do not quit.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-04`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: App State Store + Config Persistence Layer
tasks.description:
# Task 05 — App State Store + Config Persistence Layer

**Phase 1** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Create the app-wide state store and a durable local config persistence layer.

## Scope
- State store (Zustand/Redux-lite) for panels, layout, orb, connection status.
- Config persistence to local disk (layouts, presets, selected views, service settings pointers).
- Typed schema + migration-safe read/write.

## Acceptance Criteria
- State survives reload; config round-trips to disk.
- Schema typed; corrupt/missing config falls back to defaults safely.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-05`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Panel Component + Title Bar + Content Container
tasks.description:
# Task 06 — Panel Component + Title Bar + Content Container

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Build the discrete Panel window component: header/title bar + swappable content container.

## Scope
- Panel = titled frame with header (title, optional status dot, menu affordance) + content slot.
- Content is a pluggable view (by view-id); loading/empty/error states.
- Supports transition in/out (fade/slide/none).

## Acceptance Criteria
- A Panel renders any registered view by id with a title.
- Empty/loading/error states styled.
- Matches design system.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-06`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

tasks.name: 3x3 Grid Layout Manager (unified virtual canvas)
tasks.description:
# Task 07 — 3x3 Grid Layout Manager (unified virtual canvas)

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement the tiling layout manager that arranges 9 panels on a unified virtual canvas.

## Scope
- Model a virtual canvas subdivided into a 3x3 grid; panels occupy grid cells.
- Default even 3x3 with center reserved for the Orb (panel 5).
- Deterministic mapping cell<->pixel; resize-aware.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 9 panels render in a clean 3x3; center shows the Orb.
- Layout is data-driven (positions from state).
- Crisp 4K screenshot.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-07`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Edit-Layout Mode (drag/resize + snap guides)
tasks.description:
# Task 08 — Edit-Layout Mode (drag/resize + snap guides)

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Add an Edit-Layout mode letting the user move/resize panels with snapping.

## Scope
- Toggle via hotkey + on-screen menu button; drag handles + snap guides.
- Snap to grid cells and canvas edges; visual snap feedback; exit locks layout.
- Non-destructive: cancel restores prior layout.

## Acceptance Criteria
- User can move/resize; panels snap; exiting locks.
- Snap guides visible during drag.
- Cancel restores.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-08`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.description:
# Task 09 — Layout Persistence + Named Presets

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Persist layouts and support named presets recallable by the user and by Vera.

## Scope
- Persist current layout to config; reload on restart.
- Named presets (e.g. Briefing, Ops Focus, Single-Screen) save/recall.
- Expose layout.apply(preset) hook for the protocol layer.

## Acceptance Criteria
- >=2 presets save and recall correctly.
- Layout persists across restart.
- layout.apply switches presets.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-09`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Multi-Monitor Topology Awareness + Panel Focus/Fullscreen
tasks.description:
# Task 10 — Multi-Monitor Topology Awareness + Panel Focus/Fullscreen

**Phase 2** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Make the canvas display-topology-aware and add per-panel focus/fullscreen.

## Scope
- Read OS display arrangement (Electron screen API); map virtual canvas across monitors.
- Panels snap to monitor edges/bezels; grid extends across added displays.
- panel.focus expands a panel to fullscreen; panel.unfocus / orb fullscreen + return.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single display now; canvas ready to span >=2 monitors from topology.
- Any panel can go fullscreen and restore.
- Orb can take center stage and return.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-10`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: HUD Protocol Types + Validator (versioned vocabulary)
tasks.description:
# Task 11 — HUD Protocol Types + Validator (versioned vocabulary)

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement the versioned HUD command protocol as shared TypeScript types + a validator.

## Scope
- Encode the envelope + full v1 vocabulary from HUD-PROTOCOL.md (orb.*, panel.*, layout.*, speak, listen.state, brief.*, hud.*).
- Runtime validator (zod or similar): rejects unknown command, out-of-range panel, unknown view.
- Shared module usable by both bridge and HUD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every v1 command has a typed schema.
- Invalid commands rejected with structured hud.error.
- Unit tests for validation edge cases.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-11`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/*-warroom.json`, never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Bridge Service Skeleton + Gateway WS Client
tasks.description:
# Task 12 — Bridge Service Skeleton + Gateway WS Client

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Create the standalone bridge service and its client connection to the OpenClaw gateway.

## Scope
- Node/TS service, separate process; connects to gateway ws://127.0.0.1:18789.
- Reconnect/backoff; health endpoint; structured logging.
- Loads its config/secrets from ~/.openclaw/secrets; runs independently of the HUD.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge starts, connects to the gateway, survives gateway restarts (reconnect).
- Health check reports connection state.
- Bridge crash does not affect Vera or the HUD process.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-12`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: HUD<->Bridge WebSocket Channel + Command Router
tasks.description:
# Task 13 — HUD<->Bridge WebSocket Channel + Command Router

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Establish the local WebSocket channel between bridge and HUD and route validated commands.

## Scope
- Bridge exposes a local WS the HUD connects to; HUD sends events (layout edits, focus, ack/nack).
- Router validates every inbound command (task 11) then dispatches to HUD handlers.
- Ack/nack + hud.error on rejection; never silently drop.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge can push a command that the HUD renders.
- HUD emits events the bridge receives.
- Invalid command -> nack + hud.error.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-13`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. HUD renders/speaks only — no external mutating actions. Secrets loaded from `~/.openclaw/secrets/*-warroom.json`, never hardcoded.

tasks.name: Command Handlers (orb/panel/layout/speak) + Mock Data Feed
tasks.description:
# Task 14 — Command Handlers (orb/panel/layout/speak) + Mock Data Feed

**Phase 3** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement HUD-side handlers for the full command set, driven by a mock data feed.

## Scope
- Handlers: orb.state/return, panel.set/clear/focus/unfocus/highlight/update, layout.apply/save/edit, speak (stub), listen.state.
- Mock data provider so panels show plausible content before real data.
- brief.start stub sequences orb+panels for a canned briefing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every v1 command visibly affects the HUD with mock data.
- brief.start plays a mock briefing.
- Screenshots for review.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-14`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.description:
# Task 15 — Vera "War Room" Skill (war-room) Authoring

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Author the additive war-room skill for Vera per VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md (does NOT modify Vera core).

## Scope
- SKILL.md-style instructions: panel map, briefing choreography, orb-state presence, barge-in re-issue, C6 confirmation, single-source-of-truth, A5 channel/target rule.
- Skill is loaded only in War Room context; dormant otherwise.
- Delivered as an additive skill file; documents install location on the Mac mini.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Skill file complete and consistent with HUD-PROTOCOL vocabulary.
- Explicitly additive + reversible; no core edits.
- Includes the locked ops->email->calendar->flags->handoff briefing order.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-15`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: warroom_emit Tool (bridge-gated) + Registration
tasks.description:
# Task 16 — warroom_emit Tool (bridge-gated) + Registration

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement the warroom_emit tool Vera uses to emit validated HUD commands, gated on bridge availability.

## Scope
- Tool accepts an array of command objects (command+args); stamps envelope (protocol/v/id/ts).
- Sends to bridge; returns per-command ack/nack (hud.error on reject).
- Registered additively; inert/hidden when the bridge is not reachable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Vera can call warroom_emit and drive the HUD.
- Bad commands return hud.error.
- Tool inert with no bridge; Vera behaves normally.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-16`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: End-to-End Vera-Drives-HUD Integration + Resilience
tasks.description:
# Task 17 — End-to-End Vera-Drives-HUD Integration + Resilience

**Phase 4** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Wire the full A1-A4 loop and prove resilience: Vera turn -> bridge -> HUD.

## Scope
- A Vera turn drives orb.state/panel.set/panel.clear/panel.focus/layout.apply live.
- Resilience: bridge crash != Vera crash; HUD crash != Vera crash; auto-reconnect.
- Vera core verified unmodified except the additive tool/skill.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Live Vera turn manipulates the HUD end to end.
- Each process can crash/restart without taking down the others.
- Vera's other duties unaffected.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-17`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Telegram Activity Subscription + Command Fan-Out (Jon-only)
tasks.description:
# Task 18 — Telegram Activity Subscription + Command Fan-Out (Jon-only)

**Phase 5** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Make Telegram-originated Vera turns drive the room, restricted to Jon during testing (A5).

## Scope
- Bridge subscribes to Vera activity; turns emitting HUD commands render on screen.
- Text reply returns to Telegram when the request came from Telegram (C5).
- TARGET RESTRICTED to Jon 6492555042 (A5 test rule); production cutover is task 34.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 'brief me' on Telegram plays the briefing on the HUD + returns a text summary to Jon.
- 'put today's intake on the screen' updates the correct panel + confirmation.
- Only Jon's chat triggers the room.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-18`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

tasks.name: Briefing Choreography (brief.start beats) E2E
tasks.description:
# Task 19 — Briefing Choreography (brief.start beats) E2E

**Phase 5** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement the real Opening Briefing as a Vera-driven choreography over the live command set.

## Scope
- Locked order: ops -> email -> calendar -> flags -> handoff.
- Orb fullscreen open -> panel beats -> alert highlight on flags -> orb.return + handoff.
- Driven by Vera via speak + panel.* (real structure, data wired later in Phase 6).

## Acceptance Criteria
- 'brief me' runs the full choreographed sequence.
- Order matches the locked spec.
- Barge-in interrupts cleanly.

## Delivery

tasks.name: analyst-01 API Client + Bridge Service-Token Auth (break-proof)
tasks.description:
# Task 20 — analyst-01 API Client + Bridge Service-Token Auth (break-proof)

**Phase 6** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Build the analyst-01 API client and implement the break-proof bridge service-token auth (Option 1).

## Scope
- Client for the analyst-01 endpoints backing the 9 views.
- ADD an additive fail-safe middleware branch on analyst-01: runs only when Bearer==SERVICE_TOKEN from an existing secret file; maps to read-only warroom-bridge@ viewer; else UNCHANGED fallthrough.
- Constant-time compare + long random secret; guard on secret-file existence (absent => inert).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bridge reads analyst-01 with the service token.
- REGRESSION TESTS (all must pass before merge): existing Google user still authenticates; bad/absent token still 401s; bridge token reads but cannot mutate; deleting the secret reverts to current behavior.
- No change to existing dashboard auth behavior.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-20`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Global rules:** TNFG palette teal `#5BA4B5` / cyan `#00D4E0` on near-black `#04080C`, red `#D32F2F` alerts. TypeScript everywhere. Build must pass before PR. Never modify Vera's core install. tasks.name: Hybrid Refresh (polling + on-demand) + Single-Source-of-Truth Guard
tasks.description:
# Task 23 — Hybrid Refresh + Single-Source-of-Truth Guard

**Phase 6** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Add hybrid refresh (polling + on-demand) and enforce spoken==panel figures.

## Scope
- Per-panel polling (30-120s); on-demand refresh via Vera command; real-time reserved for live-ops-feed if warranted.
- Single-source guard: any figure Vera speaks and any panel shows come from the same layer.
- Backoff on source errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Polling works per panel; 'Vera, update intake' forces immediate refresh.
- Spoken figure == panel figure (spot-checked).
- Errors degrade gracefully.

## Delivery
- Create feature branch `task-23`, commit with clear messages, push, open a PR against `main`.
- Ensure the build passes locally before pushing.

**Reference docs (repo root):** SPEC.md, HUD-PROTOCOL.md, BUILD-PLAN.md, DEPENDENCIES.md, EXPERIENCE.md, VERA-WARROOM-SKILL.md, DECISIONS.md.

**Phase 7** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Implement the real-time conversational layer: turn-taking, barge-in, EOT, push-to-talk first.

## Scope
- VAD + end-of-turn detection; barge-in interrupts Vera's TTS and adapts.

- User can interrupt Vera mid-speech; she adapts.

- Latency feels conversational.

**Phase 7** · Project: War Room v2 (VERA HUD) · Repo: jwazar-TNFG/warroom-v2

## Objective
Add the 'Vera' wake word and enforce spoken confirmation for mutating actions.

## Scope
- Wake-word engine: Porcupine custom 'Vera' keyword if AccessKey available, else openWakeWord trained locally.
- Wake word activates listening with low false-trigger; same framework setting (no rebuild from push-to-talk).
- C6: outbound/mutating actions require spoken read-back + explicit 'yes'.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 'Vera' reliably wakes listening; low false-trigger.
- Read-only actions execute freely; mutating actions require 'yes'.
- Engine selection documented.
